In [1]:
import geopandas as gpd
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib.patches import Patch
from matplotlib.lines import Line2D
import os
In [2]:
folder = r"Data/CD Ecumene"
lcd_path = folder + r"/lcd_000e21a_e.shp"
lecu_path = folder + r"/lecu000e21a_e.shp"
csv_path = folder + r"/canada2024census.csv"

# Load layers
lcd = gpd.read_file(lcd_path)
lecu = gpd.read_file(lecu_path)
lecu = lecu.to_crs(lcd.crs)

# Keep only inhabited ecumene polygons (flag == 1)
lecu1 = lecu[lecu['ECUMENE'].astype(int) == 1].copy()

# Load and join population to CDs (Code -> CDUID)
pop = pd.read_csv(csv_path, encoding='latin1')
pop['Code'] = pop['Code'].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(4)
pop['Population'] = pd.to_numeric(pop['Population'])
agg = pop.groupby('Code', as_index=False)['Population'].sum()

lcd['CDUID'] = lcd['CDUID'].astype(str).str.strip().str.zfill(4)
merged = lcd.merge(agg, how='left', left_on='CDUID', right_on='Code')

# Bins and palette
merged['population_k'] = merged['Population'] / 1000.0
bin_edges = [0,20,40,100,200,np.inf]
bin_labels = ['< 20','20 to < 40','40 to < 100','100 to < 200','≥ 200']
merged['pop_bin'] = pd.cut(merged['population_k'], bins=bin_edges, labels=bin_labels, include_lowest=True)
colors = ['#fff5f0','#fcbba1','#fc9272','#fb6a4a','#cb181d']
color_map = dict(zip(bin_labels, colors))

# Intersection: restrict coloring to inhabited ecumene
inside = gpd.overlay(merged[['geometry','pop_bin']], lecu1[['geometry']], how='intersection', keep_geom_type=False)

# Plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11,9))
ax.set_axis_off()
base_fill = '#f9f8f5'  # very light off-white
merged.plot(ax=ax, facecolor=base_fill, edgecolor='#d6d6d6', linewidth=0.12, zorder=1)
for lab in bin_labels:
    sub = inside[inside['pop_bin'] == lab]
    sub.plot(ax=ax, color=color_map[lab], edgecolor='none', zorder=5)
merged.boundary.plot(ax=ax, color='black', linewidth=0.20, zorder=9)

legend_patches = [Patch(facecolor=base_fill, edgecolor='black', label='Sparsely populated (outside ecumene)')]
legend_patches += [Patch(facecolor=color_map[l], edgecolor='black', label=f'{l} (thousands)') for l in bin_labels]
ax.legend(handles=legend_patches, title='Population (thousands)', loc='lower left', fontsize=8)
ax.set_title('Canada 2024 Population Density')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('cd_pop_2024.png', dpi=600)
In [10]:
# EV charging stations overlay onto the CD+ecumene map

ev_csv = r"Data/alt_fuel_stations_ev_charging_units.csv"

# Read stations (assumes standard latitude/longitude column names)
df = pd.read_csv(ev_csv, encoding='latin1', low_memory=False)
lat_col = 'Latitude'
lon_col = 'Longitude'

# Build GeoDataFrame from lon/lat in WGS84, then project to match CDs
gdf_ev = gpd.GeoDataFrame(df, geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(df[lon_col], df[lat_col], crs='EPSG:4326'))
gdf_ev = gdf_ev.to_crs(lcd.crs)

# Clip points to Canada extent using CD boundaries
gdf_ev_ca = gpd.clip(gdf_ev, lcd)

# Plot base map again and overlay points
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11,9))
ax.set_axis_off()
base_fill = '#f9f8f5'
merged.plot(ax=ax, facecolor=base_fill, edgecolor='#d6d6d6', linewidth=0.12, zorder=1)
for lab in bin_labels:
    sub = inside[inside['pop_bin'] == lab]
    sub.plot(ax=ax, color=color_map[lab], edgecolor='none', zorder=5)
merged.boundary.plot(ax=ax, color='black', linewidth=0.20, zorder=9)

# EV points on top (smaller markers)
gdf_ev_ca.plot(ax=ax, color='#2b83ba', markersize=3, alpha=0.85, edgecolor='white', linewidth=0.15, zorder=15)

# Legend combining population bins + EV points
poly_handles = [Patch(facecolor=base_fill, edgecolor='black', label='Sparsely populated (outside ecumene)')]
poly_handles += [Patch(facecolor=color_map[l], edgecolor='black', label=f'{l} (thousands)') for l in bin_labels]
pt_handle = Line2D([0],[0], marker='o', color='w', label='EV charging station', markerfacecolor='#2b83ba', markeredgecolor='white', markersize=4)
handles = poly_handles + [pt_handle]
ax.legend(handles=handles, title='Population (thousands) + EV stations', loc='lower left', fontsize=8)
ax.set_title('Canada 2024 Population Density with EV Charging Stations Overlaid')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('cd_pop_2024_ev_chargers.png', dpi=800)
In [11]:
# Minimal loader for NHS highways from FileGDB
from fiona import listlayers
gdb_path = r"Data/highway_system.gdb"
layers = listlayers(gdb_path)
candidate = next((l for l in layers if any(k in l.lower() for k in ["nhs","route","road","highway"])) , layers[0])
hw_gdf = gpd.read_file(gdb_path, layer=candidate)
if hw_gdf.crs and lcd.crs and hw_gdf.crs != lcd.crs:
    hw_gdf = hw_gdf.to_crs(lcd.crs)
if 'type_code' not in hw_gdf.columns:
    hw_gdf['type_code'] = 1
In [12]:
# NHS Routes + EV Charging Stations (assumes highway GeoDataFrame `hw_gdf` exists)

# Classify routes using type_code
hw_gdf['nhs_class'] = hw_gdf['type_code'].astype(int).map({1:'Core Route',2:'Feeder Route',3:'Northern/Remote Route'})

# Load EV stations
ev_csv = r"Data/alt_fuel_stations_ev_charging_units.csv"
df = pd.read_csv(ev_csv, encoding='latin1', low_memory=False)
lat_col = 'Latitude'
lon_col = 'Longitude'

gdf_ev = gpd.GeoDataFrame(df, geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(df[lon_col], df[lat_col], crs='EPSG:4326'))
gdf_ev = gdf_ev.to_crs(lcd.crs)

# Clip EV points to Canada extent using lcd boundaries
gdf_ev_ca = gpd.clip(gdf_ev, lcd)

# Plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11,9))
ax.set_axis_off()
ax.set_title('Canada NHS Routes with EV Charging Stations')

# Draw census division boundaries lightly
lcd.boundary.plot(ax=ax, color='#000000', linewidth=0.25, zorder=2)

# Route colors
route_colors = {'Core Route': '#d7301f','Feeder Route': '#fc8d59','Northern/Remote Route': '#c9b458'}
for cls,lw in [('Core Route',1.2),('Feeder Route',1.0),('Northern/Remote Route',0.9)]:
    subset = hw_gdf[hw_gdf['nhs_class'] == cls]
    subset.plot(ax=ax, color=route_colors[cls], linewidth=lw, alpha=0.95, zorder=5)

# EV points
gdf_ev_ca.plot(ax=ax, color='#2b83ba', markersize=3, alpha=0.85, edgecolor='white', linewidth=0.15, zorder=10)

# Legend
handles = [
    Line2D([0],[0], color=route_colors['Core Route'], lw=2.8, label='Core Route'),
    Line2D([0],[0], color=route_colors['Feeder Route'], lw=2.8, label='Feeder Route'),
    Line2D([0],[0], color=route_colors['Northern/Remote Route'], lw=2.8, label='Northern/Remote Route'),
    Line2D([0],[0], marker='o', color='w', label='EV charging station', markerfacecolor='#2b83ba', markeredgecolor='white', markersize=5)
]
ax.legend(handles=handles, title='NHS Routes + EV Stations', loc='lower left', fontsize=8)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('canada_nhs_routes_ev.png', dpi=600)
In [ ]:
# Ensure highway classification present
hw_gdf['nhs_class'] = hw_gdf['type_code'].astype(int).map({1:'Core Route',2:'Feeder Route',3:'Northern/Remote Route'})

# Load EV data
ev_csv = r"Data/alt_fuel_stations_ev_charging_units.csv"
df_ev = pd.read_csv(ev_csv, encoding='latin1', low_memory=False)
lat_col, lon_col = 'Latitude', 'Longitude'

gdf_ev = gpd.GeoDataFrame(df_ev, geometry=gpd.points_from_xy(df_ev[lon_col], df_ev[lat_col], crs='EPSG:4326'))
gdf_ev = gdf_ev.to_crs(lcd.crs)
gdf_ev_ca = gpd.clip(gdf_ev, lcd)

# Figure
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11,9))
ax.set_axis_off()
ax.set_title('Canada 2024 Population Density + NHS Routes + EV Charging Stations')

# Base layer
base_fill = '#f9f8f5'
merged.plot(ax=ax, facecolor=base_fill, edgecolor='#d6d6d6', linewidth=0.12, zorder=1)

# Ecumene-colored population bins
for lab in bin_labels:
    sub = inside[inside['pop_bin'] == lab]
    sub.plot(ax=ax, color=color_map[lab], edgecolor='none', zorder=4)

# Boundary outline
merged.boundary.plot(ax=ax, color='black', linewidth=0.18, zorder=9)

# NHS Routes
route_colors = {'Core Route': '#d7301f','Feeder Route': '#fc8d59','Northern/Remote Route': '#c9b458'}
for cls,lw in [('Core Route',1.25),('Feeder Route',1.05),('Northern/Remote Route',0.95)]:
    subset = hw_gdf[hw_gdf['nhs_class'] == cls]
    subset.plot(ax=ax, color=route_colors[cls], linewidth=lw, alpha=0.92, zorder=12)

# EV Charging Stations
gdf_ev_ca.plot(ax=ax, color='#2b83ba', markersize=3, alpha=0.80, edgecolor='white', linewidth=0.12, zorder=20)

# Legends: population at lower-left outside; routes+EV to its right with tighter gap
population_handles = [Patch(facecolor=base_fill, edgecolor='black', label='Outside inhabited ecumene')]
population_handles += [Patch(facecolor=color_map[l], edgecolor='black', label=f'{l} (thousands)') for l in bin_labels]
leg_pop = ax.legend(handles=population_handles, title='Population (thousands)', loc='lower left', fontsize=8, frameon=True, bbox_to_anchor=(0.00, -0.02), bbox_transform=ax.transAxes)
ax.add_artist(leg_pop)

route_ev_handles = [
    Line2D([0],[0], color=route_colors['Core Route'], lw=2.6, label='Core Route'),
    Line2D([0],[0], color=route_colors['Feeder Route'], lw=2.6, label='Feeder Route'),
    Line2D([0],[0], color=route_colors['Northern/Remote Route'], lw=2.6, label='Northern/Remote Route'),
    Line2D([0],[0], marker='o', color='w', label='EV charging station', markerfacecolor='#2b83ba', markeredgecolor='white', markersize=5)
]
ax.legend(handles=route_ev_handles, title='NHS Routes + EV stations', loc='lower left', fontsize=8, frameon=True, bbox_to_anchor=(0.28, -0.02), bbox_transform=ax.transAxes)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('canada_population_routes_ev.png', dpi=800)

Saved canada_population_routes_ev.png (combined map, 800 DPI).
